content:
# corvia-adapter-agent-teams: Brainstorm Decisions

**Date:** 2026-03-28
**Doc:** docs/decisions/2026-03-28-adapter-agent-teams-brainstorm.md

Five open questions were brainstormed with deep independent analysis. Key decisions:

## Q1: Capture Before Cleanup
**Decision:** Hook-based incremental capture (primary) + periodic scan (fallback).
Hooks (TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, teammate SessionEnd) write to staging at `~/.corvia/staging/agent-teams/{team-name}/`. Staging survives cleanup. Skip TeammateIdle (too noisy). Matches claude-sessions adapter pattern exactly.

## Q2: Agent ID Correlation
**Decision:** Use teammate name as link key (names are unique, used for message routing). SessionStart hook writes mapping file at `~/.claude/sessions/.team-mapping` with `{session_id}\t{team_name}\t{agent_name}`. CorrelationIndex tries: explicit mapping > agent name in prompt > temporal overlap. Research spike needed to verify available env vars.

## Q3: Incremental Capture
**Decision:** Hybrid. TaskCreated/TaskCompleted hooks capture incrementally. Teammate SessionEnd does final sweep (inbox, config, remaining tasks). All file reads synchronous inside hook (files may be deleted before async processing). Skip TeammateIdle for capture.

## Q4: Message Chunking
**Decision:** Task-grouped chunking primary (one entry per task discussion, 5-15 entries per session). Optional LLM extraction secondary pass (decisions, findings, disagreements via classify-queue). 6-month estimate: 1,500-4,500 entries.

## Q5: Subagent Attribution
**Decision:** Rely on existing `spawned_by` chain from claude-sessions adapter. No team metadata on subagents. No direct team-to-subagent edges. Strictly hierarchical graph: team -> has_member -> teammate -> spawned_by <- subagent. Three hops within max_depth:3 default. Zero modifications to claude-sessions adapter needed.

## Blocker
One research spike needed before RFC: spin up Agent Teams and dump env vars from teammate SessionStart hook to verify what identity information is accessible.
